title: Arachnorchis longicauda
taxonomy: Arachnorchis longicauda is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it is further classified under the genus Arachnorchis. Arachnorchis longicauda is native to Australia, specifically in the states of New South Wales and Queensland. The classification of this orchid species is consistent with the APG III system.

intro: Arachnorchis longicauda is a terrestrial orchid native to Australia, also known as the Long-tailed Spider Orchid, characterized by its unique, spider-like appearance. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing the severity of infections. The bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis longicauda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Arachnorchis longicauda is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are often utilized to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
